					Theragatha				
			

			TheragāthāThag 3.11
 The Verses of Arahant Upāli (249-251)
 	
	
			

		
		
	

	
	
	

		
		
			
249. A newly ordained monk who entered the Buddha’s path out of faith, abandoning the home life, should practice right livelihood. He should be energetic and associate with noble friends.

250. A newly ordained monk who entered the Buddha’s path out of faith, abandoning the home life, should live in the midst of monks. He should learn the code of conduct well.

251. A newly ordained monk who entered the Buddha’s path out of faith, abandoning the home life, should be skilled in recognising what is allowable and unallowable. He should live without focusing on craving.

These verses were said by Arahant Upāli.

		
		
		
	
	

	

Theragāthā 3.11: The Verses of Arahant Upāli (249-251)
